sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f: iteratively search f ∈ [0,1] using parabolic interpolation or similar algorithm to maximize TWR = ∏(1 + f × trade_result/largest_loss). Position size = f × account_equity / largest_loss. Stop search when TWR begins to decrease.

source_rule_text: Use parabolic interpolation search or other algorithms to find the optimal f value between 0 and 1 that maximizes TWR (Terminal Wealth Relative), testing successive f values until TWR begins to decrease, indicating the previous f was optimal.
